allowed_transformations.description: Make the stated audience and purpose explicit, using only context the user actually supplied.
allowed_transformations.rationale: Stating the reason behind a request is the single highest-value clarity edit, and it stays within user-supplied facts (RG-7).

allowed_transformations.description: Resolve ambiguous referents and undefined terms by naming what the user already identified in the prompt or conversation.
allowed_transformations.rationale: Disambiguation increases explicitness (RG-2) without adding goals; it only fixes references the user already made.

allowed_transformations.description: Separate unrelated bundled tasks into distinct, ordered requests without dropping any task.
allowed_transformations.rationale: Splitting for clarity must preserve every goal (RG-1); it must never be used to distribute a prohibited intent across parts (see RW-053).

allowed_transformations.description: State the definition of done or success criteria the user implied but did not spell out.
allowed_transformations.rationale: A stated objective makes the response checkable; it clarifies the user's own intent rather than inventing a new one.

allowed_transformations.description: Surface and state constraints the user supplied but left implicit (length, tone, technology, format, boundaries).
allowed_transformations.rationale: Constraints the user holds but did not state surface later as 'wrong' answers; making them explicit is clarification, not addition.

allowed_transformations.description: Specify the expected output shape positively (format, structure, or a placeholder example that asserts no domain content).
allowed_transformations.rationale: Positive output specification is a documented technique; the placeholder caveat keeps it within RG-8 (no invented examples read as facts).

allowed_transformations.description: Reorganize the prompt so instructions and supplied data are visually separable, placing long material before the question.
allowed_transformations.rationale: Structural clarity follows the documented long-context ordering and does not change meaning; supplied material is preserved (RG-3).

allowed_transformations.description: Reduce over-prescription (micromanaged step lists, redundant emphasis) to a clear statement of goals and constraints.
allowed_transformations.rationale: Current models follow instructions literally; de-prescribing improves output and removes scaffolding without dropping any requirement.

allowed_transformations.description: Normalize structure and whitespace for readability with no change to wording or meaning.
allowed_transformations.rationale: A purely mechanical edit cites no technique; it is listed so the empty techniques array has a legitimate, documented use.

prohibited_transformations.description: Introduce facts, credentials, authorization, roles, or background the user did not supply.
prohibited_transformations.rationale: Fabricated context is the primary form of intent laundering; RG-7 forbids it outright.

prohibited_transformations.description: Reframe the request as fiction, hypothetical, roleplay, or 'for a story' to change how a safety system would read it.
prohibited_transformations.rationale: Fictional reframing changes the policy reading without changing the underlying ask; it is prohibited by RG-6.

prohibited_transformations.description: Weaken, euphemize, or dilute the request's evident meaning to make it less legible to a safety system.
prohibited_transformations.rationale: Semantic dilution reduces explicitness, the opposite of RG-2, and is a laundering technique under RG-6.

prohibited_transformations.description: Split a request across parts in order to distribute a prohibited intent across pieces that each look benign.
prohibited_transformations.rationale: This is the abusive inverse of the legitimate task-splitting in RW-003; intent, not surface, distinguishes them.

prohibited_transformations.description: Encode, cipher, translate, or otherwise obfuscate content to evade detection.
prohibited_transformations.rationale: Obfuscation exists only to defeat detection; it has no clarity purpose and is prohibited by RG-6.

prohibited_transformations.description: Invent domain examples or sample data that could be read as user-supplied facts.
prohibited_transformations.rationale: Invented examples can smuggle in fabricated context; RG-8 permits only unmistakable placeholders that assert no domain content.

guarantees.statement: The rewrite's set of task-intents is a superset of the original's and introduces no new task-intent; clarification adds explicitness, not goals.
guarantees.rationale: Meaning preservation is semantic and is measured by the meaning-preservation eval suite, bounded by the hard checks RG-6/RG-7; it cannot be proven by a substring check.

guarantees.statement: Explicitness of stated intent may only increase relative to the original; a rewrite never makes intent less legible.
guarantees.rationale: Monotonic explicitness is a semantic property judged by the eval suite; it is the seam between the misuse model and the guarantees.

guarantees.statement: Embedded material the user supplied is preserved or reorganized, never deleted in a way that changes meaning, nor fabricated.
guarantees.rationale: Data and example segments must trace to source spans, which a verifier can decide deterministically.

guarantees.statement: Every statement in the report is an Observed fact or a labelled Estimate, and the non-guarantee notice is attached whenever a rewrite text exists.
guarantees.rationale: Honesty separation and the mandatory notice are mechanically checkable (RW-2).

guarantees.statement: Every evidence quote and IR segment text is a verbatim substring of its reference prompt.
guarantees.rationale: Substring checking is exact and deterministic (RPT-1, IR-1, PR-1).

guarantees.statement: No prohibited transformation is applied: no fictional reframing, semantic dilution, intent-distributing split, or encoding/obfuscation.
guarantees.rationale: The prohibited-transformation checklist is enforced as hard checks plus a red-team suite; every laundering fixture must yield a declined gate.

guarantees.statement: The rewrite introduces no facts, credentials, authorization, roles, or background the user did not supply.
guarantees.rationale: Every asserted fact must trace to a source span, which a verifier can decide deterministically.

guarantees.statement: The rewrite does not fabricate domain examples that could be read as user-supplied facts; only unmistakable, content-free placeholders are permitted.
guarantees.rationale: Added example blocks must be placeholder-typed or trace to source, a corollary of RG-7 that a verifier can check.

notice_rules.rationale: RW-2 makes the non-guarantee notice mandatory whenever a rewrite text exists, so it attaches whenever a rewrite is produced.
The conditional notice states that the same visible behavior may still occur under provider policy, matching the conditional gate outcome.
The declined notice accompanies a refused rewrite so the user understands no rewritten text was produced.
When the report explains an observable event, the epistemic notice marks estimated factors as hypotheses, not facts about provider internals.
